title: A randomised active-controlled trial to examine the effects of an online mindfulness intervention on executive control, critical thinking and key thinking dispositions in a university student sample
headline: A randomised active-controlled trial to examine the effects of an online mindfulness intervention on executive
summary: A randomized controlled trial of mindfulness / meditation examined brief & app-based practice. However, no significant effects of group allocation were observed for either primary or secondary measures. No evidence was found to suggest that engaging in guided mindfulness practice for 6 weeks using the online intervention method applied in this study improves critical thinking performance.
keyFindings: The aim of this study was to test this claim by investigating the effects of an online mindfulness intervention on executive function, critical thinking skills and associated thinking dispositions.
However, no significant effects of group allocation were observed for either primary or secondary measures.
No evidence was found to suggest that engaging in guided mindfulness practice for 6 weeks using the online intervention method applied in this study improves critical thinking performance.
limitations: Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.
The practice studied (mindfulness / meditation) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
